sets.passage: During photosynthesis, green plants convert sunlight, water, and carbon dioxide into glucose, releasing oxygen as a byproduct that most animals depend on to breathe.
sets.explain_zh: 目标词 photosynthesis：可由短文学术语境唯一还原（首字母及部分字母已给出）。

sets.passage: Isaac Newton realized that the same gravity pulling a falling apple toward the ground also holds the Moon in its orbit, preventing it from drifting off into space.
sets.explain_zh: 目标词 gravity：可由短文学术语境唯一还原（首字母及部分字母已给出）。

sets.passage: Before running an experiment, a careful scientist first states a testable hypothesis, a tentative explanation that the collected data will later either support or overturn.
sets.explain_zh: 目标词 hypothesis：可由短文学术语境唯一还原（首字母及部分字母已给出）。

sets.passage: In an early Athenian democracy, ordinary male citizens gathered in the public assembly to debate and vote directly on new laws, rather than obeying the will of a single king.
sets.explain_zh: 目标词 democracy：可由短文学术语境唯一还原（首字母及部分字母已给出）。

sets.passage: A healthy wetland ecosystem ties together water, soil, insects, fish, and wading birds in one interdependent web, so that removing a single species can disturb every other member.
sets.explain_zh: 目标词 ecosystem：可由短文学术语境唯一还原（首字母及部分字母已给出）。

sets.passage: Pressed into ancient layers of rock, a single fossil can reveal the shape, size, and approximate age of a creature that died millions of years before the first humans walked the Earth.
sets.explain_zh: 目标词 fossil：可由短文学术语境唯一还原（首字母及部分字母已给出）。

sets.passage: Combining careful digging with laboratory analysis, archaeology allows researchers to reconstruct how prehistoric peoples farmed, traded goods, and buried their dead, long before any written records survived.
sets.explain_zh: 目标词 archaeology：可由短文学术语境唯一还原（首字母及部分字母已给出）。

sets.passage: Although far too small to see without a microscope, helpful bacteria live inside the human gut, where many species break down food and manufacture vitamins the body cannot make alone.
sets.explain_zh: 目标词 bacteria：可由短文学术语境唯一还原（首字母及部分字母已给出）。

sets.passage: Before coins were minted, communities bartered goods directly; introducing a standard currency finally let people assign one fixed, agreed value to products as different as grain, cloth, and cattle.
sets.explain_zh: 目标词 currency：可由短文学术语境唯一还原（首字母及部分字母已给出）。

sets.passage: Ancient farmers dug long channels to carry river water across dry land, a method of irrigation that let their crops flourish far from the natural banks of the river.
sets.explain_zh: 目标词 irrigation：可由短文学术语境唯一还原（首字母及部分字母已给出）。

sets.passage: A national court usually has no jurisdiction over crimes committed inside another country, so the two governments must sign a formal treaty before one can prosecute a fugitive from the other.
sets.explain_zh: 目标词 jurisdiction：可由短文学术语境唯一还原（首字母及部分字母已给出）。

sets.passage: Ecologists call the sea otter a keystone species because its steady hunting keeps sea-urchin numbers low, which in turn allows entire underwater kelp forests to survive and thrive.
sets.explain_zh: 目标词 keystone：可由短文学术语境唯一还原（首字母及部分字母已给出）。

sets.passage: Because sunlight strikes the equator almost straight down, regions at low latitude stay warm throughout the year, while lands near the poles receive only weak, sharply slanting rays.
sets.explain_zh: 目标词 latitude：可由短文学术语境唯一还原（首字母及部分字母已给出）。

sets.passage: Deep beneath a volcano, molten rock called magma slowly collects inside a hidden chamber; once the pressure grows unbearable, it bursts through the surface and flows out as glowing lava.
sets.explain_zh: 目标词 magma：可由短文学术语境唯一还原（首字母及部分字母已给出）。

sets.passage: The human brain contains tens of billions of neurons, specialized cells that fire electrical signals and pass them across microscopic gaps to control thought, memory, sensation, and movement.
sets.explain_zh: 目标词 neurons：可由短文学术语境唯一还原（首字母及部分字母已给出）。

sets.passage: When bare iron is left exposed to oxygen in damp air, slow oxidation forms a flaky, reddish-brown layer of rust that steadily eats into and weakens the metal.
sets.explain_zh: 目标词 oxidation：可由短文学术语境唯一还原（首字母及部分字母已给出）。

sets.passage: To keep a dangerous outbreak from spreading, health officials may place newly infected travelers under quarantine, isolating them completely until they can no longer pass the illness to anyone else.
sets.explain_zh: 目标词 quarantine：可由短文学术语境唯一还原（首字母及部分字母已给出）。

sets.passage: During a severe recession, a nation's total output shrinks for many months, companies lay off workers, and anxious consumers spend less, which only deepens the wider economic downturn.
sets.explain_zh: 目标词 recession：可由短文学术语境唯一还原（首字母及部分字母已给出）。

sets.passage: A well-preserved museum specimen lets modern biologists examine the exact coloring, bone structure, and body proportions of an animal that a naturalist collected more than a century ago.
sets.explain_zh: 目标词 specimen：可由短文学术语境唯一还原（首字母及部分字母已给出）。

sets.passage: Relying on taxonomy, biologists sort every living thing into a nested hierarchy of groups, such as kingdom, class, order, and species, based on the physical features organisms share.
sets.explain_zh: 目标词 taxonomy：可由短文学术语境唯一还原（首字母及部分字母已给出）。

sets.passage: In his famous book, the philosopher described an imaginary utopia with no poverty, crime, or greed, a flawless society so perfect that it could never actually exist in the real world.
sets.explain_zh: 目标词 utopia：可由短文学术语境唯一还原（首字母及部分字母已给出）。

sets.passage: By exposing the immune system to a harmless fragment of a virus, a vaccine trains the body to recognize that exact pathogen and destroy it quickly during any future infection.
sets.explain_zh: 目标词 vaccine：可由短文学术语境唯一还原（首字母及部分字母已给出）。

sets.passage: Every drop of rain that falls within a river's watershed eventually drains into the same main channel, which means pollution released far upstream can harm communities living far downstream.
sets.explain_zh: 目标词 watershed：可由短文学术语境唯一还原（首字母及部分字母已给出）。

sets.passage: By carefully rotating their crops and adding nitrogen fertilizer, the farmers steadily raised the annual grain yield of each field, harvesting far more wheat from exactly the same area of soil.
sets.explain_zh: 目标词 yield：可由短文学术语境唯一还原（首字母及部分字母已给出）。

sets.passage: As a major branch of biology, zoology investigates the anatomy, behavior, and classification of animals, ranging from nearly invisible microscopic organisms to the enormous blue whales of the open ocean.
sets.explain_zh: 目标词 zoology：可由短文学术语境唯一还原（首字母及部分字母已给出）。

sets.passage: Charles Darwin argued that evolution through natural selection favors organisms whose inherited traits best fit their surroundings, gradually reshaping entire species across countless generations of survival and reproduction.
sets.explain_zh: 目标词 evolution：可由短文学术语境唯一还原（首字母及部分字母已给出）。

sets.passage: Prehistoric painters ground colored minerals into a fine pigment, then mixed the powder with animal fat so the bright hues would cling to the rough stone walls of their caves.
sets.explain_zh: 目标词 pigment：可由短文学术语境唯一还原（首字母及部分字母已给出）。

sets.passage: In classical physics, an object's momentum equals its mass multiplied by its velocity, which explains why a heavy freight truck is far harder to stop than a bicycle moving at the same speed.
sets.explain_zh: 目标词 momentum：可由短文学术语境唯一还原（首字母及部分字母已给出）。

sets.passage: When developers drain a coastal marsh, they wipe out the fragile habitat that frogs, turtles, and wading birds depend upon for their food, shelter, and safe breeding grounds.
sets.explain_zh: 目标词 habitat：可由短文学术语境唯一还原（首字母及部分字母已给出）。

sets.passage: A chemical catalyst speeds up a reaction without being used up itself, which is exactly why factories add only a tiny amount of platinum to accelerate otherwise sluggish industrial processes.
sets.explain_zh: 目标词 catalyst：可由短文学术语境唯一还原（首字母及部分字母已给出）。
